responseMessageContents.text: The repo `ibb142/rork-native-app-development` doesn't exist publicly or is private, so I can't access it. The actual full app is in `ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest`, which is already synced into this workspace.

Short answer: **no** — that repo isn't your full app. I just checked GitHub directly:

- `ibb142/rork-native-app-development` → **404 Not Found**. It either doesn't exist or is set to private, so nothing can be cloned from it.
- `ibb142/rork-global-real-estate-invest` → **exists and is public**. This is your real full app repo — the one already synced into this workspace (backend with 111 API modules, the 254-screen Expo app, the landing page, and deploy configs). Your live Render backend at api.ivxholding.com also deploys from this repo.

So your full app is already here and connected end to end — no clone needed.

If `rork-native-app-development` is a **private** repo you just created and you want me to pull it in, I'd need access — either make it public temporarily or tell me what's in it and I'll sync it. Otherwise, everything you have lives in `rork-global-real-estate-invest`.

Want me to verify the workspace is still in full sync with that repo's latest commit?
